subject: Product Management
title: Failure Is Data
description: A short lesson to help you apply Failure Is Data.

hook.text: Every product failure in this course taught the industry something. Failure isn't the opposite of success — it's the input.

buildup.text: Google Glass paved the way for Apple Vision Pro. WebTV led to Roku and Apple TV. Vine's DNA lives inside TikTok.

discovery.text: The best product leaders study failures more than successes because failures reveal boundaries that success obscures.

twist.text: The real danger isn't failing — it's failing and not learning. Organizations that punish failure create cultures that hide it.

climax.text: Amazon's Jeff Bezos said: 'If you're going to invent, you're going to fail. They're inseparable twins.'

punchline.text: Every failure is data. The only waste is not learning from it.

quiz.question: What is the most important lesson about product failure?
options: Failure is valuable data — the only waste is not learning from it
All failures should be avoided at all costs
Successful companies never experience product failures
